recipes.title: Asado
recipes.description: Argentina's iconic barbecue: an array of beef cuts and sausages slow-grilled over wood embers, served with chimichurri.
steps: Build a wood or charcoal fire and let it burn down to glowing embers; the grill (parrilla) should be 25–30 cm above them.
Make chimichurri: combine parsley, garlic, vinegar, oil, oregano, pepper flakes, and salt; rest 1 hour.
Salt all meats generously with coarse rock salt 30 minutes before grilling.
Start the chorizos and morcillas on the grill — they take about 20 minutes total, turning occasionally.
Place tira de asado bone-side down for 25–30 minutes, then flip and cook another 15 minutes.
Add vacío fat-side down for 20 minutes, then flip and cook 15 more minutes for medium-rare.
Rest meats 5 minutes, slice across the grain, and serve with chimichurri and crusty bread.

recipes.title: Empanada
recipes.description: Argentine baked turnovers with flaky pastry and savory beef filling spiced with cumin, paprika, and green olives.
steps: Make pastry: rub fat into flour with salt, add warm water, and knead briefly into a smooth dough. Rest 1 hour.
Brown beef quickly, drain excess fat, and stir in onion, green onion, cumin, paprika, and pepper flakes.
Cook 5 minutes until onions soften, season with salt, and chill the filling thoroughly.
Stir in chopped boiled eggs and olives.
Roll dough thin and cut into 12 cm rounds. Place 1 tbsp filling in each.
Moisten edges, fold over into half-moons, and crimp with the classic repulgue twist.
Brush with beaten egg and bake at 200°C (400°F) for 18–20 minutes until golden.

recipes.title: Milanesa
recipes.description: Argentine breaded beef cutlets pounded thin and pan-fried until golden — a weeknight staple, often topped with ham and cheese.
steps: Pound beef slices between sheets of plastic to about 5 mm thick.
Beat eggs with milk, garlic, parsley, and salt in a shallow dish.
Spread breadcrumbs in a second shallow dish.
Soak each cutlet in egg mixture 5 minutes, then press firmly into breadcrumbs to coat both sides.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high until shimmering.
Fry milanesas 2–3 minutes per side until deep golden and crisp; do not crowd the pan.
Drain on paper towels, season with extra salt if needed, and serve with lemon wedges and a green salad.

recipes.title: Choripán
recipes.description: Argentina's beloved street sandwich: grilled chorizo butterflied into crusty bread and slathered with chimichurri.
steps: Whisk together parsley, garlic, vinegar, olive oil, oregano, pepper flakes, and salt to make chimichurri. Rest 1 hour.
Light a grill or charcoal fire and let coals burn down until glowing.
Prick chorizos a few times to prevent bursting.
Grill chorizos over medium heat for 12–15 minutes, turning often, until cooked through and charred.
Butterfly each chorizo lengthwise without cutting all the way through, then return to the grill cut-side down for 2 minutes.
Split bread rolls and lightly toast them on the grill.
Place a chorizo in each roll, top generously with chimichurri, and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Provoleta
recipes.description: Argentine grilled provolone disc with crispy edges and gooey center, finished with oregano and chili — the asado opener.
steps: Use a thick (about 2 cm) round of aged provolone — provoleta-specific cheese works best.
Place cheese in a small cast-iron skillet or directly on a clean, hot grill grate.
Drizzle the top with olive oil and sprinkle with oregano and chili flakes.
Grill over medium-high heat for 3–4 minutes until the bottom forms a deep golden crust.
Carefully flip with a spatula and grill another 2 minutes until the underside is crisp and the inside is molten.
Slide onto a wooden board.
Serve immediately with crusty bread to scoop up the cheese.

recipes.title: Locro
recipes.description: Hearty Andean stew of corn, beans, squash, and various meats — Argentina's traditional dish for May 25 and Independence Day.
steps: Soak hominy and beans separately in cold water overnight, then drain.
In a large pot, brown pork, beef, ribs, and chorizo in oil.
Add onion and garlic, cook until soft, then stir in paprika and cumin.
Add hominy, beans, and 3 liters of water. Bring to a boil, reduce, and simmer 2 hours.
Add squash and continue cooking for 1 more hour until everything is meltingly tender and the broth thickens.
Mash some squash and beans against the side of the pot to thicken the locro further.
For the topping, fry green onions in oil with paprika to make a quitucho. Spoon over each bowl.

recipes.title: Dulce de Leche
recipes.description: Argentina's velvety milk caramel — slowly cooked sweetened milk that becomes thick, glossy, and amber.
steps: Combine milk, sugar, and split vanilla bean in a heavy, wide pot.
Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves.
Once simmering, stir in baking soda — it will foam, which helps develop the caramel color.
Reduce heat to low and cook uncovered, stirring occasionally, for about 1.5 hours.
After the first hour, stir more frequently to prevent sticking as the mixture thickens.
Continue until the dulce de leche is thick, glossy, and a deep amber color, and coats the back of a spoon.
Remove vanilla bean and transfer to a clean jar. Cool completely; it will thicken further on cooling.

recipes.title: Alfajor
recipes.description: Two delicate buttery cookies sandwiched with dulce de leche and rolled in coconut — Argentina's national sweet.
steps: Cream butter with powdered sugar until pale and fluffy.
Beat in egg yolks one at a time, then lemon zest and vanilla.
Sift cornstarch, flour, and baking powder, then fold into the butter mixture into a soft dough.
Wrap and chill 30 minutes. Roll dough on a floured surface to 5 mm thick and cut 4 cm rounds.
Bake at 170°C (340°F) for 10–12 minutes — they should remain pale, not brown.
Cool fully on a rack.
Sandwich with a generous dollop of dulce de leche, then roll the exposed edges in shredded coconut.

recipes.title: Matambre
recipes.description: Argentine flank steak roll stuffed with carrots, peppers, eggs, and herbs, then braised or grilled and sliced into pinwheels.

ingredients.name: matambre (flank or skirt steak), butterflied thin

steps: Lay the butterflied steak flat. Rub with garlic, oregano, pepper flakes, salt, and olive oil.
Layer spinach across the meat, then arrange carrots and red pepper strips lengthwise.
Place whole boiled eggs in a row down the center, scatter parsley over everything.
Roll the steak tightly, starting from the long edge, and tie firmly with kitchen twine at intervals.
Sear the roll in a heavy pot until browned all over.
Pour in beef broth, cover, and braise at 160°C (320°F) for 90 minutes until tender.
Rest 15 minutes, snip twine, and slice into 2 cm pinwheels. Serve warm or chilled.

recipes.title: Bife de Chorizo
recipes.description: Argentina's prized strip-loin steak, thick-cut and grilled to a perfect crust over charcoal embers.

ingredients.name: bife de chorizo (strip loin steaks), 4 cm thick

steps: Bring steaks to room temperature 30 minutes before grilling.
Build a hot charcoal fire and let it burn down to glowing embers.
Make chimichurri by combining parsley, garlic, vinegar, olive oil, oregano, and a pinch of salt.
Salt the steaks generously with coarse rock salt right before grilling.
Place over high heat and grill 5–6 minutes per side for medium-rare, turning once for clean grill marks.
Rest steaks loosely tented 8 minutes — this is essential for juicy meat.
Slice thick or serve whole with chimichurri and grilled vegetables.

recipes.title: Pastel de Papas
recipes.description: Argentine shepherd's pie with seasoned ground beef, olives, and boiled eggs blanketed in creamy mashed potatoes.
steps: Boil potatoes until tender, then mash with butter, milk, salt, and pepper into a smooth puree.
Sauté onion and pepper until soft, add garlic, cumin, paprika, and tomato paste; cook 2 minutes.
Brown ground beef with the aromatics until cooked, season generously with salt, then cool slightly.
Stir olives into the meat mixture.
Spread the meat in a baking dish, layer sliced boiled eggs over the top.
Cover completely with mashed potatoes, dragging a fork over the surface for texture, and dust with parmesan.
Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 25 minutes until golden and bubbling at the edges.

recipes.title: Carbonada
recipes.description: Argentine winter stew of beef, sweet corn, peaches, and butternut squash, traditionally served inside a hollowed pumpkin.
steps: Brown beef cubes in olive oil in a large pot until deeply caramelized, then remove.
Sauté onion and bell pepper until soft, add garlic, paprika, and cumin, then tomatoes.
Cook the sofrito 5 minutes until rich and reduced.
Return beef and pour in broth. Simmer covered for 45 minutes until beef begins to tenderize.
Add potatoes, sweet potato, and squash; simmer 20 more minutes.
Add corn and chopped peaches; simmer a final 10 minutes until everything is tender and the broth thick and slightly sweet.
Adjust seasoning and serve hot, ideally inside a roasted hollowed pumpkin for celebrations.
